project.name: audio-layer
project.description: Record without a meeting bot. Leave with a transcript, live summary, key points, action items, follow-up context, and searchable memory your AI tools can use.

design.brandSummary: Layers Design System v1.0, Paper Calm. Calm paper surfaces, structured controls, mint primary accent, violet and blue support colors, canonical aperture logo.
design.interactionStyle: Clear task-first flows with visible feedback and recoverable states.
referenceSystems: Layers Design System v1.0 - Paper Calm
design.accessibility: WCAG AA contrast, keyboard reachability, visible focus, and reduced-motion support.

productValidation.customer: Founders, product teams, and GTM teams that run frequent planning, customer, and sales meetings and need decisions, owners, follow-ups, and context available immediately after the call.
productValidation.problem: Important decisions and action items get scattered across transcripts, manual notes, Slack, email, calendars, and AI chats. Existing meeting bots can feel intrusive, miss context, and rarely produce structured outputs that teams can search, share, or reuse in AI tools.
productValidation.currentWorkaround: Manual notes, Zoom or Meet transcripts, Otter or Fathom style meeting bots, Notion or Docs, Tana style knowledge graphs, Slack recaps, and ad hoc copy-paste into ChatGPT or Claude.
productValidation.proposedSolution: Record without a meeting bot. Leave with a transcript, live summary, key points, action items, follow-up context, and searchable memory your AI tools can use.
productValidation.pricing: Free $0, Core $20 per user per month, Pro $30 per user per month. Pricing must stay easy to change before launch.
productValidation.distribution: Download-first launch from the Layer One website with platform-aware Mac and Windows links and mobile app-store links as builds become available. Pair with founder-led outreach to product teams, AI productivity users, and early customer conversations.
productValidation.timing: Teams are adopting AI tools faster than their meeting context can move with them. Better speech models, cheaper summarization, and desktop or mobile distribution make a private meeting memory layer practical now.
productValidation.constraints: Do not commit secrets. Keep provider keys in env. Ship AssemblyAI today, add Deepgram Live when the API key is supplied, keep Stripe Free/Core/Pro configurable, and verify primary routes with browser proof.

integrations.label: Vercel AI Gateway
notes: Default model-provider path. Local dev may use AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY or a pulled VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN.

integrations.label: Supabase
notes: Preferred database adapter, but starter dashboards must keep filesystem fallbacks.

integrations.label: AssemblyAI transcription
notes: Direct paid APIs should emit recordApiUsage()/trackedFetch() events.

integrations.label: Stripe payments
notes: Operational cost and revenue accounting are separate; track API usage locally when useful.

integrations.label: Resend email
notes: Email sends should record local usage events if they affect product cost.

requirements.label: Vercel AI Gateway authentication
requirements.reason: Required for local model calls through the Vercel AI Gateway.

nextSteps: Put real secrets in .env.local or pull them with `vercel env pull .env.local --yes`.
Run `pnpm run starter:doctor` to validate setup health without printing secret values.
Run `pnpm plan -- "<first feature>"` before feature work.
Open `/control-plane` after starting the dev server.
